name: Accept
description: Informs the server about the types of data that can be sent back. It is a MIME type.

name: Accept-Encoding
description: Indicates the content encodings the client can understand, such as gzip or br (brotli).

name: Accept-Language
description: Indicates the natural language and locale that the client prefers.

name: Authorization
description: Contains the credentials to authenticate a user agent with a server, usually after a 401 response.

name: Cache-Control
description: Holds directives for caching in both requests and responses. These are used to control caching throughout the request/response chain.

name: Content-Type
description: Indicates the media type of the resource or the data being sent in the body.

name: Content-Length
description: Indicates the size of the message body in bytes.

name: Cookie
description: Contains stored HTTP cookies previously sent by the server with the Set-Cookie header.

name: Set-Cookie
description: Sent by the server to instruct the client to store a cookie.

name: ETag
description: An identifier for a specific version of a resource. Allows caches to be more efficient and saves bandwidth.

name: Host
description: Specifies the host and port number of the server to which the request is being sent. Required in all HTTP/1.1 requests.

name: If-None-Match
description: Makes the request conditional; the server returns the resource only if the ETag does not match.

name: Location
description: Indicates the URL to redirect a page to. Only has meaning when served with a 3xx (redirection) or 201 (created) response.

name: Origin
description: Indicates where a fetch originates from. Sent with CORS requests and POST requests.

name: Access-Control-Allow-Origin
description: Indicates whether the response can be shared with resources with the given origin.

name: Access-Control-Allow-Methods
description: Specifies the HTTP methods allowed when accessing a resource in response to a preflight request.

name: Referer
description: Contains the address of the previous web page from which a link to the currently requested page was followed.

name: Retry-After
description: Indicates how long the user agent should wait before making a follow-up request. Used with 429 and 503 responses.

name: Server
description: Contains information about the software used by the origin server to handle the request.

name: Strict-Transport-Security
description: Informs browsers that the site should only be accessed using HTTPS, and that any future attempts to access it using HTTP should automatically be converted to HTTPS.

name: User-Agent
description: A characteristic string that lets servers and network peers identify the application, operating system, vendor, and version of the requesting client.

name: WWW-Authenticate
description: Defines the authentication method that should be used to gain access to a resource. Returned with a 401 Unauthorized response.

name: X-Content-Type-Options
description: Prevents MIME type sniffing by instructing browsers to follow the declared Content-Type.

name: X-Frame-Options
description: Indicates whether or not a browser should be allowed to render a page in a frame, iframe, or object. Used to prevent clickjacking.

name: Content-Security-Policy
description: Allows web site administrators to control resources the user agent is allowed to load for a given page. Helps prevent XSS and data injection attacks.
